parameters.content:
## Modules 8-9: Final Resume + Memory
Final resume generated from approved data. Preferences stored for future personalization.
BR-7 Resume Validation | BR-10/11/12 Preference Scoring | BR-3 Versioning | BR-18 Audit Log | BR-13 Summary Dashboard

FIX (Issue 3): user_preferences DataTable CREATE removed. Memory consolidated to staticData only.
application_summary + audit_log remain in DataTable (correct).

FIX (Bug 5): Removed erroneous connections from DataTable CREATE nodes into Store nodes.
CREATE nodes are one-time schema setup; Store nodes receive from Prepare nodes directly.

## Modules 5-7: Content Generation
BR-1 Smart priority-based review | BR-2 Memory Retrieval | BR-9 Keyword Density
FIX: Cover Letter + Email prompts use $node references for job/ats data.
FIX: Merge Content Outputs is 3 inputs.

FIX (Issue 1): user_id now derived from email field (if provided) with name fallback.

## Module 4: ATS Matching
FIXED: ATS → App Recommendation ONLY.
FIXED: Enrich ATS Context passes deterministic data to Suggestions Engine.
BR-4 Deterministic Skill Match | BR-5 Experience Calc | BR-8 Dedup | BR-6 Confidence | BR-15 Recommendation

## Modules 2-3: Parallel Analysis
Job and resume analyzed independently and simultaneously.
Guard nodes catch empty/failed LLM output before merge.

FIX (Bug 1): Job Structure Parser jsonSchemaExample expanded to ALL 12 fields.
FIX (Bug 2): Resume Structure Parser jsonSchemaExample expanded to ALL 13 fields.
FIX (Bug 3): Guard nodes now output job_output / resume_output namespaced keys
             to prevent 'output' key collision in Combine Job & Resume Analysis merge.
             Skill & Experience Analyzer updated to read namespaced keys.

## Module 1: Job Input
Upload job description PDF/DOCX/TXT and resume PDF/DOCX/TXT.
BR-17 Input Validation — checks for empty/missing files.

FIX (Issue 1): Added optional 'Email / Username' field to form.
Used as stable user_id if provided. Falls back to name if empty.

parameters.formDescription: Upload your job description and resume to get AI-powered application materials.

values.fieldLabel: Email or Username (optional — used to remember your preferences across sessions)

values.formSubmittedText: Processing your application... Results will be displayed shortly.

parameters.text:
=You are a Job Description Parser.

Return a single JSON object:

{
  "role":"",
  "job_level":"",
  "employment_type":"",
  "industry":"",
  "location":"",
  "experience_required":"",
  "education_required":"",
  "skills_required":[],
  "skills_preferred":[],
  "tools_and_technologies":[],
  "responsibilities":[],
  "keywords":[],
  "soft_skills":[]
}

Rules:

role:
- Exact job title.

job_level:
- Intern | Junior | Mid-Level | Senior | Lead | Manager | Unknown

employment_type:
- Full-Time | Part-Time | Contract | Internship | Unknown

industry:
- Explicit or inferred sector.

location:
- As written.
- Hybrid → "Hybrid — City"

experience_required:
- Exact experience requirement.

education_required:
- Minimum education requirement.

skills_required:
- Mandatory skills only.
- Deduplicate.
- Exclude soft skills.

skills_preferred:
- Nice-to-have skills only.

tools_and_technologies:
- All technologies, languages, frameworks, tools, cloud, databases.

responsibilities:
- Extract up to 10 responsibilities using original wording.

keywords:
- ATS terms.
- Include technologies, methodologies, certifications, domain terms.
- Deduplicate.
- 5–20 when available.

soft_skills:
- Only explicitly mentioned interpersonal skills.

Job Description:
{{ $json.text }}

parameters.jsonSchemaExample: {"role":"Frontend Developer","job_level":"Mid-Level","employment_type":"Full-Time","industry":"Technology","location":"Remote","experience_required":"2+ years","education_required":"Bachelor's degree in Computer Science or equivalent","skills_required":["React","TypeScript","REST APIs"],"skills_preferred":["GraphQL","Docker"],"tools_and_technologies":["React","TypeScript","Node.js","Git"],"responsibilities":["Build and maintain frontend applications","Collaborate with design and backend teams"],"keywords":["React","TypeScript","Frontend Development","REST APIs","Agile"],"soft_skills":["Communication","Team collaboration"]}

parameters.text:
=You are a Resume Parser.

Return:

{
  "name":"",
  "email":"",
  "phone":"",
  "location":"",
  "linkedin":"",
  "portfolio":"",
  "summary":"",
  "skills":[],
  "experience":[
    {
      "title":"",
      "company":"",
      "start":"",
      "end":"",
      "description":""
    }
  ],
  "projects":[
    {
      "name":"",
      "technologies":[],
      "description":""
    }
  ],
  "education":[
    {
      "degree":"",
      "institution":"",
      "year":""
    }
  ],
  "certifications":[],
  "languages":[]
}

Rules:

- Extract only explicit information.
- Never infer skills.

skills:
- Technical/professional skills only.
- Deduplicate.
- Canonical names preferred.

experience:
- Extract all roles.
- Preserve title/company.
- Current role → "Present".
- Description: concise summary from resume content only.

projects:
- Extract only listed projects.
- Technologies must be explicitly mentioned.

education:
- Extract all entries.

certifications:
- Include issuing body when available.

languages:
- Spoken/written languages only.

Resume:
{{ $json.text }}

parameters.jsonSchemaExample: {"name":"Varshit Madisetti","email":"varshit@email.com","phone":"+1-555-0100","location":"San Francisco, CA","linkedin":"linkedin.com/in/varshit","portfolio":"varshit.dev","summary":"Full-stack developer with 3 years of experience building scalable web applications.","skills":["React","TypeScript","Node.js","JavaScript","Python","SQL"],"experience":[{"title":"Full Stack Developer","company":"Fresh Prints","start":"07/2023","end":"Present","description":"Built React apps and REST APIs serving 50k users"},{"title":"Junior Developer","company":"Startup XYZ","start":"01/2022","end":"06/2023","description":"Developed internal tooling in Python and JavaScript"}],"projects":[{"name":"Colorization Transformer","technologies":["Python","PyTorch","OpenCV"],"description":"Deep learning model for automatic image colorization"},{"name":"Zero-Day Attack Detection","technologies":["Python","Scikit-learn","Pandas"],"description":"ML pipeline for network intrusion detection"}],"education":[{"degree":"B.Sc. Computer Science","institution":"University of Technology","year":"2021"}],"certifications":["AWS Certified Developer – Associate (Amazon)"],"languages":["English","Hindi"]}

parameters.text:
=You are an ATS Scoring Engine.

Return:

{
  "final_score":0,
  "breakdown":{
    "skill_match":0,
    "experience_match":0,
    "semantic_match":0,
    "formatting":0
  },
  "missing_skills":[],
  "matched_skills":[],
  "summary":"",
  "confidence":0
}

Deterministic values (must be used exactly):

{{ JSON.stringify($json.deterministic) }}

Mappings:

skill_match = deterministicSkillScore
experience_match = experienceMatchScore
matched_skills = matchedSkills
missing_skills = missingSkills

Compute:

semantic_match (0-100):
- Domain relevance
- Transferable experience
- Relevant projects
- Do not count matched skills again

formatting (0-100):
- Resume structure quality
- Presence of summary, skills, experience, projects, education
- Date consistency and readability

penalty = missingSkills.length × 5

Formula:

final_score =
round(
(skill_match*0.40)+
(experience_match*0.30)+
(semantic_match*0.20)+
(formatting*0.10)-
penalty
)

Clamp 0-100.

confidence (0-100):
- Based on how explicit the evidence is.

summary:
- 1–2 factual sentences.
- Mention skill match, experience fit and major gap.

Job:
{{ JSON.stringify($json.job) }}

Resume:
{{ JSON.stringify($json.resume) }}

parameters.jsonSchemaExample: {"final_score":78,"breakdown":{"skill_match":80,"experience_match":70,"semantic_match":75,"formatting":90},"missing_skills":["REST APIs"],"matched_skills":["React","TypeScript"],"summary":"Good skill match with minor experience gap.","confidence":85}

parameters.text:
=You are a Resume Improvement Engine.

Return:

{
  "resume_suggestions":[
    {
      "text":"",
      "priority":"high|medium|low",
      "category":"skills|experience|keywords|formatting|projects"
    }
  ]
}

Input:

ATS:
{{ JSON.stringify($json.output) }}

Deterministic:
{{ JSON.stringify($json.deterministic) }}

Verdict:
{{ $json.verdict }}

Rules:

Generate 5–8 suggestions.

Mandatory:

1. For each missing skill:
- priority: high
- category: skills
- Suggest explicitly adding evidence of that skill if possessed.

2. For each lowDensityKeyword:
- priority: medium
- category: keywords
- Suggest increasing truthful usage.

Additional suggestions as needed:

- Experience gap
- Formatting issues
- Missing projects
- Reordering matched skills

Requirements:

- 20–60 words each.
- Specific and actionable.
- No duplicates.
- Never suggest lying or inventing experience.

assignments.name: ats
assignments.value: ={{ $node["ATS Matching Engine"].json.output !== undefined ? $node["ATS Matching Engine"].json.output : $node["ATS Matching Engine"].json }}

parameters.text:
=Write a tailored cover letter using only the supplied data.

Job:
{{ JSON.stringify($node["Prepare Generation Payload"].json.job) }}

Resume:
{{ JSON.stringify($node["Prepare Generation Payload"].json.resume) }}

ATS:
{{ JSON.stringify($node["Prepare Generation Payload"].json.ats) }}

Memory:
{{ JSON.stringify($node["Smart Review Processor"].json.memory_context) }}

Requirements:

- 150–180 words
- 4 paragraphs

Paragraph 1:
- State role and company (if available)
- Mention strongest relevant qualification

Paragraph 2:
- Connect experience to requirements
- Mention at least 2 matched skills
- Reference a real project or role
- Use only stated metrics

Paragraph 3:
- Explain alignment with role/company

Paragraph 4:
- Interview availability and sign-off

Rules:

- First person
- Professional and direct
- No invented facts
- No subject line

Avoid:
"I am writing to apply"
"I am passionate"
"I am excited to"
"I would be a great fit"
"Please find attached"
"To whom it may concern"

=Write a recruiter outreach email.

Candidate:
{{ JSON.stringify($node["Prepare Generation Payload"].json.resume) }}

Job:
{{ JSON.stringify($node["Prepare Generation Payload"].json.job) }}

ATS:
{{ JSON.stringify($node["Prepare Generation Payload"].json.ats) }}

Format:

Subject: Application for [role] — [candidate]

[blank line]

Intro:
- Candidate name
- Applied role
- Strongest qualification

Body:
- Mention 2–3 matched skills
- Connect to real role/project

Closing:
- Request interview

Sign-off:

Best regards,
[Name]
[Email]

Rules:

- Body 60–100 words
- Professional
- No fluff
- No invented facts

Avoid:
"I hope this email finds you well"
"Please find attached"
"I am writing to express my interest"

=You are an ATS Resume Generator.

Return:

{
  "header":"",
  "summary":"",
  "skills":[],
  "experience":[],
  "projects":[],
  "education":[]
}

Use only:

Job:
{{ JSON.stringify($json.job) }}

Resume:
{{ JSON.stringify($json.resume) }}

Accepted Suggestions:
{{ JSON.stringify($json.accepted_suggestions) }}

Memory:
{{ JSON.stringify($json.memory_context) }}

Rules:

header:
- Name | Email | Phone | Location | LinkedIn
- Omit missing fields

summary:
- 2–4 sentences
- Max 60 words
- Based only on resume

skills:
- Resume skills only
- Deduplicate
- Matched skills first
- Max 20

experience:
Format:

[Title] · [Company] · [Start]–[End]
[bullet]
[bullet]

Bullets:
- Start with:
Built, Developed, Designed, Implemented, Engineered, Delivered, Optimised, Refactored, Integrated, Automated, Reduced, Improved, Led, Mentored, Deployed, Migrated, Architected, Tested, Debugged, Configured
- Rewrite existing content only
- No invented metrics

projects:
[Name] — [tech stack]
description

education:
[Degree] · [Institution] · [Year]

Apply accepted suggestions only when supported by resume data.

Never add:
- Skills
- Roles
- Projects
- Dates
- Metrics
- Education entries

that do not exist in the source resume.

parameters.jsonSchemaExample: {"header":"Varshit Madisetti | varshit@email.com | +1-555-0100 | San Francisco, CA | linkedin.com/in/varshit","summary":"Experienced Frontend Developer with 3+ years building scalable React applications.","skills":["React","TypeScript","REST APIs","Node.js","Python"],"experience":[{"title":"Full Stack Developer","company":"Fresh Prints","start":"07/2023","end":"Present","bullets":["Built React apps serving 50k users","Integrated REST APIs reducing latency by 30%"]}],"projects":[{"name":"Colorization Transformer","tech":"Python, PyTorch","description":"Deep learning model for image colorization"}],"education":[{"degree":"B.Sc. Computer Science","institution":"University of Technology","year":"2021"}]}
